signals.idea: Harden the Solana payment subsystem against the attack vectors found by the adversarial security review. Fix P0: git history secret audit, prompt-injection hardening in admin_chat (remove LLM-sourced wallet addresses from initiate_payment tool; wallets must come from wallet_registry keyed by mentioned Discord user), null recipient_discord_id confirmation bypass (fail-closed). Fix P1: wallet swap window between test and final payment (verify execute_payment uses stored recipient_wallet string; reject or warn on upsert_wallet during active intents), idempotency slot reuse after forced failure (warn/DM on producer_ref collision with prior failed row having different wallet), per-payment and rolling daily USD cap on admin-triggered payouts enforced in PaymentService (default $500/payment, $2000/day/guild, exceeding goes to manual_hold), admin DM on successful admin_chat payouts over threshold (default $100, differentiated from failure DMs). Fix P2: log audit for secret leakage, document RLS posture at top of sql/payments.sql. Already using Helius so RPC attacks out of scope. Two wallets already split into solana_grants and solana_payouts providers. Think carefully about wallet-swap and idempotency — they are subtle and easy to half-fix. Goal is end-to-end hardening.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: One remaining `must` is still broader than the concrete plan steps: the criterion that 'Cap-breach, collision (Branch A insert and Branch B reject), and null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM via on_cap_breach or equivalent path' is not backed by a corresponding null-recipient DM step. Step 1 only logs and returns `None`, and the current caller paths in `payment_cog`, `admin_chat_cog`, and `grants_cog` do not define a single uniform admin-DM mechanism for that rejection.
Confirmation flow: null-recipient rejection is not actually wired to a uniform admin-DM path even though a `must` success criterion claims it is.

resolved_flags.concern: Payments caps: Recurring debt: the revised amount_token fix still keeps derived USD out of the stored payment row, so admin-chat token-denominated payouts do not reliably participate in the rolling daily USD cap or the success-DM threshold.
resolved_flags.resolution: Fixed the single root cause behind all 8 open flags: derived USD was being kept local to the cap check instead of persisted into the stored payment row. Step 8 now explicitly stamps `record['amount_usd']` and `record['token_price_usd']` (and the mirrored request_payload fields) when USD is derived from `amount_token * token_price_usd` for a capped provider — and only in that branch, so uncapped providers and is_test paths keep their existing `amount_usd=None` behavior. This makes the rolling-24h helper (Step 7, which sums stored amount_usd) and the success-DM gate (Step 9, which reads payment.get('amount_usd')) both see the real value for admin-chat amount_token-only payouts end-to-end. Verified safety of existing tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token` at tests/test_admin_payments.py:684-709 uses uncapped provider "solana" and asserts `provider.price_calls == 0`, so our derivation branch (gated on capped_providers) never fires and the test stays green. Same for test_social_publish_service.py:496, which uses uncapped "solana_native". Added three new tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd` (pins the persisted derivation), `test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none` (regression pin for the existing behavior on uncapped providers), `test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the rolling helper), and `test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the success-DM gate). Updated assumptions to state that derived USD is persisted when the provider is capped. Updated the end-to-end cap-enforcement success criterion to explicitly require persistence so the `must` matches what the plan actually proves. Added a new invariant forbidding amount_usd stamping for uncapped/is_test paths. No other structural changes; prior fixes to exception-based upsert_wallet blocking, index-aware collision branching, control-flow ordering, and PaymentCog construction-site handling remain unchanged.

resolved_flags.concern: Wallet registry updates: The proposed `upsert_wallet` blocked-path sentinel is only handled in admin chat, but `grants_cog` is another live caller and would treat a truthy sentinel dict as a successful wallet record.
resolved_flags.resolution: Resolved all 12 open significant flags. (1) Cap enforcement now covers amount_token-only callers (admin_chat final payout): derives USD from provider.get_token_price_usd() and fail-closes to manual_hold if price is unavailable. Added two new tests pinning that path. (2) upsert_wallet blocking is now exception-based (WalletUpdateBlockedError) and both live callers — admin_chat._handle_wallet_received AND grants_cog._start_payment_flow — catch it explicitly, eliminating the truthy-sentinel trap. (3) Collision handling is now index-aware with two explicit branches: Branch A inserts a fresh manual_hold row only when every prior row is failed/cancelled (index permits); Branch B returns None without attempting an insert when a prior row still holds a unique-index slot, and the admin DM fires in both cases. Added a new Branch-B test. (4) Step 5's control flow is fixed: the existing-wallet lookup now runs before the guard, and the sample code matches the real call order. (5) PaymentCog success-DM config is now read inside __init__ from env so both construction sites (main.py:233 and the setup() hook at payment_cog.py:503) stay in sync without touching either caller. (6) Test glue expansion is explicit: inline fakes in test_admin_payments.py and test_social_publish_service.py are named as the sites where has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs must land. (7) Criteria quality: demoted git-history-audit and log-audit from must to should (they are memo tasks, not diff-verifiable); added must criteria for the two new token-path cap tests and the Branch-B collision test. No scope creep; the plan remains aligned with the original P0/P1/P2 brief.

resolved_flags.concern: Payment idempotency: The collision path over-assumes it can insert a new `manual_hold` row for any prior-wallet mismatch, but the active unique index still blocks inserts when the prior row is not `failed` or `cancelled`.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised Step 8 and Step 9 together against the real admin-chat final payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`. resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The collision path in Step 6 assumes it can create a fresh `manual_hold` row whenever any prior `(producer, producer_ref, is_test)` row has a different wallet, but `sql/payments.sql:126-128` still enforces uniqueness for every status except `failed` and `cancelled`. If the differing prior row is `pending_confirmation`, `queued`, `processing`, `submitted`, `confirmed`, or `manual_hold`, the insert cannot succeed, so the plan needs an explicit non-insert branch instead of assuming a new held row can always be created.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?: The proposed `{'__error__': 'active_payment_or_intent'}` return value does not match the surrounding `DatabaseHandler` convention, which is overwhelmingly `Optional[Dict]` or `bool` with `None`/`False` on failure across `src/common/db_handler.py`. That divergence is risky here because current callers already treat any dict as success.

Added three new tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd` (pins the persisted derivation), `test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none` (regression pin for the existing behavior on uncapped providers), `test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the rolling helper), and `test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the success-DM gate). Updated assumptions to state that derived USD is persisted when the provider is capped. Updated the end-to-end cap-enforcement success criterion to explicitly require persistence so the `must` matches what the plan actually proves. Added a new invariant forbidding amount_usd stamping for uncapped/is_test paths. No other structural changes; prior fixes to exception-based upsert_wallet blocking, index-aware collision branching, control-flow ordering, and PaymentCog construction-site handling remain unchanged.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Not all supporting locations are aligned on the amount_token path yet. Step 8 derives USD locally in `PaymentService.request_payment`, Step 7's rolling helper sums stored `payment_requests.amount_usd`, and Step 9's success-DM gate reads `payment.get('amount_usd')`; unless the derived USD is persisted or recomputed in the downstream sites too, the feature is still only partially wired across the repository.
resolved_flags.resolution: Fixed the single root cause behind all 8 open flags: derived USD was being kept local to the cap check instead of persisted into the stored payment row. Step 8 now explicitly stamps `record['amount_usd']` and `record['token_price_usd']` (and the mirrored request_payload fields) when USD is derived from `amount_token * token_price_usd` for a capped provider — and only in that branch, so uncapped providers and is_test paths keep their existing `amount_usd=None` behavior. This makes the rolling-24h helper (Step 7, which sums stored amount_usd) and the success-DM gate (Step 9, which reads payment.get('amount_usd')) both see the real value for admin-chat amount_token-only payouts end-to-end. Verified safety of existing tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token` at tests/test_admin_payments.py:684-709 uses uncapped provider "solana" and asserts `provider.price_calls == 0`, so our derivation branch (gated on capped_providers) never fires and the test stays green. Same for test_social_publish_service.py:496, which uses uncapped "solana_native". Added three new tests: `test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd` (pins the persisted derivation), `test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none` (regression pin for the existing behavior on uncapped providers), `test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the rolling helper), and `test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd` (end-to-end wiring through the success-DM gate). Updated assumptions to state that derived USD is persisted when the provider is capped. Updated the end-to-end cap-enforcement success criterion to explicitly require persistence so the `must` matches what the plan actually proves. Added a new invariant forbidding amount_usd stamping for uncapped/is_test paths. No other structural changes; prior fixes to exception-based upsert_wallet blocking, index-aware collision branching, control-flow ordering, and PaymentCog construction-site handling remain unchanged.

resolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the actual admin payout caller and the downstream consumer. `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365` still creates final payouts with `amount_token` only, while the planned success-DM gate in `PaymentCog` keys off `amount_usd`; with the revised assumption that derived USD stays local instead of being stored, the real caller shape is still not handled end-to-end.

This makes the rolling-24h helper (Step 7, which sums stored amount_usd) and the success-DM gate (Step 9, which reads payment.get('amount_usd')) both see the real value for admin-chat amount_token-only payouts end-to-end. resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: There is still no test that an amount_token-only admin-chat payout contributes to the rolling-24h USD total or that a confirmed amount_token-only payout can cross the success-DM threshold. Because the revised plan keeps derived USD out of the stored row, those two real behaviors can still be wrong while every newly listed test passes.

Because the revised plan keeps derived USD out of the stored row, those two real behaviors can still be wrong while every newly listed test passes.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 3. Weighted score trajectory: 19.0 -> 13.0 -> 3.0. Plan deltas: 61.3%, 36.8%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 18. Open significant flags: 2.

items.criterion: All existing tests in tests/test_admin_payments.py, tests/test_scheduler.py, and tests/test_social_publish_service.py pass unchanged (default None caps preserve existing PaymentService construction; amount_usd stays None for uncapped providers).
New test test_confirm_rejects_null_recipient_discord_id passes: confirm_payment with recipient_discord_id=None returns None and logs WARN.
New test verifying privileged_override=True still bypasses the null-recipient check passes.
New test test_confirm_rejects_mismatched_user passes: confirm_payment with a non-matching confirmed_by_user_id returns None.

New test test_request_payment_per_payment_cap_manual_holds passes: amount_usd over per-payment cap lands the row as manual_hold with cap-breach last_error; grants provider unaffected; on_cap_breach invoked.
New test test_request_payment_amount_token_path_cap_breach passes: capped provider, amount_token with no amount_usd, derived USD (amount_token * token_price_usd) exceeds cap → manual_hold.

New test test_request_payment_amount_token_path_stamps_amount_usd passes: capped provider, amount_token within cap, provider returns a valid price → the created row has `amount_usd == amount_token * price` and `token_price_usd == price` (NOT None). Pins that derived USD is persisted end-to-end.

New test test_request_payment_amount_token_path_missing_price_holds passes: capped provider, amount_token path, provider returns None/0 for price → manual_hold with last_error='cap check unavailable'.

New test test_request_payment_amount_token_uncapped_provider_preserves_none passes: uncapped provider ('solana'), amount_token path → result['amount_usd'] is None AND provider price lookup is NOT called. Regression pin against existing amount_token test behavior.

New test test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_manual_holds passes: rolling 24h aggregation across non-test solana_payouts rows in (pending_confirmation, queued, processing, submitted, confirmed) drives the cap; grants unaffected.

New test test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_sees_derived_usd passes: prior confirmed capped-provider row whose amount_usd was stamped via the derivation path is correctly summed by get_rolling_24h_payout_usd and a follow-up amount_token request counts against the daily cap accordingly. Pins end-to-end rolling-cap flow for admin-chat payouts.
New test test_slot_reuse_collision_detected_after_failure passes: only prior rows are failed/cancelled with a different wallet → fresh manual_hold row is inserted (Branch A) and on_cap_breach fires.

New test test_slot_reuse_collision_blocked_when_prior_active passes: prior row is pending_confirmation/queued/etc with a different wallet → request_payment returns None without attempting an insert (Branch B), the prior row is NOT replaced, and on_cap_breach fires.
New test test_slot_reuse_same_wallet_creates_fresh_row_after_failure passes: only terminal prior rows with matching wallet → new pending_confirmation row is created.
New test test_idempotent_return_for_nonterminal passes: non-terminal prior row with matching wallet is returned unchanged.

New test test_upsert_wallet_raises_during_active_intent passes: upsert_wallet raises WalletUpdateBlockedError while a non-terminal payment_requests OR admin_payment_intents row exists; both admin_chat._handle_wallet_received AND grants_cog._start_payment_flow catch it and surface user-facing messages.
New test test_upsert_wallet_unblocked_after_terminal passes: upsert proceeds when only terminal rows exist AND when the incoming address matches the stored address even while an active intent exists.

New test test_admin_success_dm_over_threshold passes: confirmed, non-test, solana_payouts, amount_usd >= threshold triggers _dm_admin_payment_success; under-threshold does not; failure DM path is unchanged.

New test test_admin_success_dm_sees_derived_usd passes: a confirmed payment whose amount_usd came from the Step 8 derivation path (amount_token-only capped caller) reliably triggers _dm_admin_payment_success. Pins end-to-end success-DM wiring for admin-chat payouts.
New test test_execute_payment_uses_stored_wallet passes: execute_payment passes payment['recipient_wallet'] (frozen) to provider.send even when wallet_registry has been updated.
New test test_initiate_payment_tool_rejects_wallet_address_arg passes: wallet_address/recipient_wallet keys in params are popped and ignored; wallet is resolved exclusively via db_handler.get_wallet.
PaymentService constructor accepts per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers, and on_cap_breach kwargs; all default to None so existing construction sites remain compatible.
Cap enforcement in PaymentService runs only in request_payment, never in execute_payment. Scheduler path is untouched.

Cap enforcement covers BOTH amount_usd-supplying callers AND amount_token-only callers end-to-end: for capped providers in the amount_token path, the derived USD is PERSISTED into record['amount_usd'] and record['token_price_usd'] (and their request_payload mirrors) BEFORE create_payment_request, so the rolling-24h aggregation and the success-DM threshold both operate on the real value. Missing price → manual_hold.

For uncapped providers (or is_test=True), request_payment does NOT stamp amount_usd on token-denominated requests. Existing tests that assert `amount_usd is None` for the uncapped 'solana' and 'solana_native' providers stay green, and provider.get_token_price_usd() is not called in that path.
Cap-breach, collision (Branch A insert and Branch B reject), and null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM via on_cap_breach or equivalent path. No silent rejection.

Collision handling is index-aware: Branch A (prior rows all failed/cancelled) inserts a fresh manual_hold row; Branch B (any prior row in a non-excluded status) returns None without attempting an insert.
upsert_wallet blocked path raises WalletUpdateBlockedError (not a truthy sentinel). Both admin_chat._handle_wallet_received and grants_cog._start_payment_flow catch the exception.
upsert_wallet's existing-wallet lookup runs BEFORE the has_active_payment_or_intent guard. Identical-address upserts and upserts with no prior row are never blocked.
_dm_admin_payment_success is a new sibling method with a distinct prefix ('✅ **Payment Completed**') and no 'requires manual review' line. _dm_admin_payment_failure is unchanged.
PaymentCog reads success-DM threshold and provider set from env inside __init__ with defaults ($100, 'solana_payouts'), so both construction sites stay in sync without modification.

main.py wires per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers=('solana_payouts',), and on_cap_breach into PaymentService from env vars with defaults $500/$2000 and logs effective caps at startup.
sql/payments.sql has a top-of-file comment block documenting the RLS posture. No ALTER TABLE changes.
Admin success DM is provider-scoped and threshold-gated; solana_grants payouts never trigger admin success DMs.

Inline fake-db glue in tests/test_admin_payments.py, tests/test_social_publish_service.py, and tests/conftest.py is extended additively with has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs (no rewrites of existing tests).
pytest tests/ runs green end-to-end.
New cap/collision/DM code paths redact wallets via existing _redact_wallet helpers and never log env values or key material.
has_active_payment_or_intent uses the same non-terminal predicates as existing helpers — no ad-hoc drift.
Inline comment at the collision-detection + idempotent-return reorder explains WHY terminal rows must not short-circuit the early return.
Rolling-24h cap helper includes a comment documenting the residual burst-race window AND the upstream-persistence dependency for amount_token-only capped callers.
Git history secret audit commands from Step 3 are run and findings recorded in the review memo.
Log audit grep from Step 11 is performed and findings recorded.
Plan output explicitly states that the P0 prompt-injection premise was already substantially satisfied by prior work.
No unrelated refactors or test rewrites beyond what the new behavior strictly requires.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: One remaining `must` is still broader than the concrete plan steps: the criterion that 'Cap-breach, collision (Branch A insert and Branch B reject), and null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM via on_cap_breach or equivalent path' is not backed by a corresponding null-recipient DM step. Step 1 only logs and returns `None`, and the current caller paths in `payment_cog`, `admin_chat_cog`, and `grants_cog` do not define a single uniform admin-DM mechanism for that rejection.
unresolved_flags.evidence: One remaining `must` is still broader than the concrete plan steps: the criterion that 'Cap-breach, collision (Branch A insert and Branch B reject), and null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM via on_cap_breach or equivalent path' is not backed by a corresponding null-recipient DM step. Step 1 only logs and returns `None`, and the current caller paths in `payment_cog`, `admin_chat_cog`, and `grants_cog` do not define a single uniform admin-DM mechanism for that rejection.

unresolved_flags.concern: Confirmation flow: null-recipient rejection is not actually wired to a uniform admin-DM path even though a `must` success criterion claims it is.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Step 1 only tightens `confirm_payment` to log and return `None`. The revised plan does not add an `on_cap_breach`-style callback or any other shared admin-DM mechanism for null-recipient rejections, while the `must` criteria still say that null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM.
